perturbation.original_text: "LMG Services" means the support, hosting and other services, functions and responsibilities provided or to be provided by LMG pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "LMG Services" means the support and other services provided or to be provided by LMG pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term "hosting" has been removed from the definition of "LMG Services." This creates a contradiction in Section 3.4, where LMG is obligated to provide hosting services. By removing "hosting" from the definition of LMG Services, the definition of LMG Services no longer includes hosting, thus making section 3.4 contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Hosting Services. LMG shall host the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software at its existing data center facility in Petaluma, California for the period specified in Exhibit A. Except for downtime for scheduled maintenance, LMG shall make the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software available to users between 7:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Notice of scheduled maintenance shall be provided to TAG via email at least ten (10) days before the scheduled maintenance. LMG shall be financially and operationally responsible for the hosting environment including maintenance, repair, replacement and upgrade, and the performance, availability, reliability, compatibility and interoperability of the LMG Tools, CSC Software, Other Third Party Software and hosting environment. LMG shall provide the hosting services through a dedicated telecommunications connection to TAG. The equipment, connectivity and other items located at LMG's existing data center facility in Petaluma, California that is described in Exhibit C to the Administrative Services and Teaming Agreement are included within the hosting environment and access to be provided by LMG under this Section 3.4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes "hosting" from the definition of "LMG Services." The contradicted text (Section 3.4) explicitly states "LMG shall host the LMG Tools". By removing "hosting" from the definition of "LMG Services," the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted section, creating an in-text contradiction, because hosting is no longer necessarily considered part of the defined services.

perturbation.original_text: LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the employment of the LMG employees identified in Exhibit B ("Retained Employees") for the estimated retention period specified in Exhibit B or such other period as TAG may reasonably request ("Retention Period"). LMG shall cause them to devote the same amount of time and attention to the performance of the services under this Agreement as they presently devote to such services. In all events, LMG shall retain a sufficient number of qualified personnel to perform the LMG Services.
perturbation.changed_text: LMG shall cause them to devote the same amount of time and attention to the performance of the services under this Agreement as they presently devote to such services. In all events, LMG shall retain a sufficient number of qualified personnel to perform the LMG Services.
perturbation.explanation: The clause that states 'LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the employment of the LMG employees identified in Exhibit B ("Retained Employees") for the estimated retention period specified in Exhibit B or such other period as TAG may reasonably request ("Retention Period").' has been removed. By removing this clause there is no clear mention on if the Retained Employees are expected to stay for the whole duration of the agreement. This is an in-text contradiction, because now the following clause refers to retain a sufficient number of qualified personnel for the LMG Services, however with no clause on how long they are to be retained for. This creates an inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Retained LMG Personnel. LMG shall cause them to devote the same amount of time and attention to the performance of the services under this Agreement as they presently devote to such services. In all events, LMG shall retain a sufficient number of qualified personnel to perform the LMG Services. The Retained Employees shall be employees of LMG for all purposes. LMG shall be solely responsible for funding and distributing benefits under the benefit plans in which the Retained Employees participate and for paying any compensation and remitting any income, disability, withholding and other employment taxes for such Retained Employees. For clarity, the foregoing shall not affect TAG's obligation to reimburse LMG for the costs specifically identified as reimbursable by TAG during the period, and under the terms and conditions, set forth in Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated LMG's obligation to retain specific employees for a specified period ('Retention Period'). The changed text removes this obligation, leading to a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' that refers to 'Retained LMG Personnel'. The Contradicted text assumes a defined retention period and obligations tied to those retained employees, whereas the changed text removes the foundation for those obligations, as it no longer explicitly states or guarantees a retention period for these individuals. Removing this creates an inconsistency with the assumption of retained employees.

perturbation.original_text: LMG shall provide training to TAG with respect to the use of the LMG Tools, Other Third Party Software and Documentation as reasonably requested by TAG.
perturbation.changed_text: LMG shall provide basic familiarization to TAG with respect to the use of the LMG Tools, Other Third Party Software and Documentation.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'training' with 'basic familiarization' in Section 3.3 creates an in-text contradiction with Section 6.4. While 3.3 now offers only a superficial introduction, Section 6.4 assumes a more comprehensive knowledge transfer during termination assistance, implying a level of training that is no longer explicitly guaranteed. This makes the assistance during termination not as effective, due to the training on how to use the tools not being effective
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination Assistance shall include the following: LMG shall provide all such information and assistance as may be necessary for TAG to transition off using the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software or to install and implement the same. LMG shall provide TAG with an extract of the Customer Data, including, without limitation, all policyholder and New Customer data. TAG shall be permitted to undertake, without interference from LMG, to hire any Retained Employees. LMG shall waive its rights, if any, under contracts with such personnel restricting the ability of such personnel to be recruited or hired by TAG; provided that counter-offers and making available positions posted through LMG's placement system and generally available to other LMG employees shall not be prohibited under this Section. TAG shall have reasonable access to such personnel for interviews and recruitment. TAG's License to the LMG Tools and subject to the terms of the applicable third party license, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software and Documentation shall survive the termination for the period specified in Exhibit A, and LMG shall provide copies of all Documentation relevant to such license which is in LMG' possession. At TAG's request, LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to assign to TAG its license for the CSC Software and any third party licenses for Other Third Party Software, and TAG shall assume the obligations under such licenses that relate to periods after such date. LMG shall also provide all Documentation relevant to such licenses which is in LMG's possession. At TAG's request (i) assign to TAG leases and other contracts for some or all of the leased equipment included in the hosting environment or otherwise used to provide the LMG Services, and TAG shall assume the obligations under such leases that relate to periods after such date; and (ii) sell to TAG, at LMG's then current book value, some or all of such items owned by LMG. LMG shall also provide all Documentation relevant to such item which is in LMG's possession. LMG shall obtain any necessary rights and thereafter make available to TAG, pursuant to reasonable terms and conditions, any third party services then being utilized by LMG in the performance of the LMG Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated 'training' on LMG Tools. The changed text replaces this with 'basic familiarization.' Section 6.4, Termination Assistance, requires LMG to provide information and assistance for TAG to transition off LMG Tools, implying a level of knowledge derived from comprehensive training. 'Basic familiarization' is insufficient to provide the 'necessary' assistance for transition, creating a direct conflict. This constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction as it undermines the effectiveness of the termination assistance.
